mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon. This is a general indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns following periods of strong RSAFS growth are not significantly positive, or if the correlation between RSAFS growth and SPX returns is consistently negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and lower corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as investors price in recessionary fears.
evidence_refs: fact:historical recessions often preceded by rising unemployment
failure_condition: If a rise in UNRATE by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months is not followed by negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days in more than 60% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is high and inflation (CPIAUCSL) is also elevated, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy environment aimed at curbing inflation. This combination typically leads to higher borrowing costs and reduced economic activity, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% and CPIAUCSL annual growth is above 3.0% are consistently followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns, or if the interaction term shows no predictive power (p-value > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts (HOUST) during a late-cycle expansion indicates a weakening in a key interest-rate sensitive sector, often preceding broader economic slowdowns. This signals reduced future demand and negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as investors react quickly to leading indicators.
evidence_refs: fact:housing as a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If a month-over-month decline in HOUST exceeding 10% during a late-cycle expansion is not followed by negative S&P 500 5-day returns in at least 70% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing and mining sectors, indicating strong supply-side capacity and demand for goods. This positive economic momentum supports corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward returns are consistently negative or uncorrelated with positive INDPRO growth over a 5-year rolling window, indicating a breakdown in the supply-side to equity market link.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates aggressive monetary tightening by the central bank, typically in response to inflationary pressures. This raises the cost of capital, dampens economic activity, and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If a monthly increase in FEDFUNDS of more than 0.5 percentage points is not followed by negative S&P 500 20-day returns in at least 65% of cases during high inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) and a declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) signifies a robust and tightening labor market. This indicates strong economic health, boosting consumer confidence and corporate revenues, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 0.1% MoM AND UNRATE declines > 0.1pp MoM
evidence_refs: fact:strong labor market supports economic growth
failure_condition: If periods of simultaneous PAYEMS growth above 0.1% and UNRATE decline above 0.1pp are not followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns in at least 70% of instances, or if the interaction term lacks statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating GDP growth (GDPC1) following a period of contraction signals a strong economic recovery. This rebound in overall demand and productivity leads to improved corporate earnings and investor sentiment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as markets anticipate future growth.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth QoQ > 0.5% after two consecutive quarters of negative growth
failure_condition: If a positive GDPC1 growth rate after two negative quarters does not lead to positive S&P 500 5-day returns in at least 75% of post-recession recovery periods.
